sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.2 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Is it true that every non-discrete group topology (in an abelian group) can be strengthened up to a maximal complete group topology? { V.\,I.\,Arnautov} No, not always, since under the assumption of the Continuum Hypothesis there exist non-complete maximal topologies (V.\,I.\,Arnautov, E.\,G.\,Zelenyuk, Ukrain. Math.~J., { 43}, no.\,1 (1991), 15--20).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
